title: Fore-edge painting — painting the fanned edge
sources: Cyril Davenport, The Book — Its History and Development (1907) — chapter on decorative treatments including fore-edge painting history
notes: Classic hidden-painting technique: text block fanned and held in a jig, painted flat, then pressed closed so the image is hidden. Covers both the display-only technique (fan while holding) and the more demanding disappearing variant (fan one way, paint; fan the other way, paint). Safety: cutting preamble not required; no sharp tools after initial trimming.
